name: Entre Túmulos Iberos y Ermita de nuestra señora de La Misericordia — circular desde Arens de Lledó
region_intro: Mountain biking around Arens De Lledó offers diverse natural landscapes within the Matarraña region, situated on a mountainous slope alongside the Algars River. The terrain features a mix of vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and Mediterranean forests, including lush pine forests. The area is characterized by its varied elevation changes, from gentle riverside tracks along the Algars River to more challenging mountainous trails. The presence of the Val de Zafán Greenway, a converted railway line, provides flat and scenic routes ideal for cycling. Best mountain bike trails around Arens De Lledó The most popular mountain bike trail is La Vía verde de Zafán (La Vía de Tus Sueños) — ida y vuelta desde Cretas, an easy 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic Val de Zafán Greenway, offering a smooth ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Entre Túmulos Iberos y Ermita de nuestra señora de La Misericordia — circular desde Arens de Lledó, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. This circular route combines natural landscapes with historical sites like Iberian tumuli and a hermitage. Local mountain bikers also love the Calaceite, La Fresneda y Ermita de Santa Ana y Los Santos — circular por la comarca de Matarraña, a 40.9 miles (65.9 km) trail leading through the Matarraña region, often completed in about 5 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Arens De Lledó is defined by its Algars River valley paths, extensive Mediterranean forests, and the accessible Val de Zafán Greenway. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more difficult regional loops. The routes in Arens De Lledó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arens De Lledó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Arens De Lledó?
A: Arens De Lledó offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 85 routes available. These range from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountainous loops, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Arens De Lledó?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes mountain biking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the varied landscapes, while summer allows for combining rides with refreshing dips in natural bathing areas along the Algars River, such as El Galeró. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to ride year-round, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Arens De Lledó has approximately 10 easy mountain bike routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995914542/la-via-verde-de-zafan-la-via-de-tus-suenos-ida-y-vuelta-desde-cretas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vía verde de Zafán&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option, following a converted railway line that offers a flat and scenic ride. These routes often follow tracks along the Algars River with minimal elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Arens De Lledó?
A: The terrain around Arens De Lledó is diverse. You'll find gentle riverside tracks along the Algars River, extensive Mediterranean forests, and more challenging mountainous trails with varied elevation changes. The Val de Zafán Greenway provides flat and scenic sections, while other routes traverse vineyards, olive groves, and almond trees.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995915541/entre-tumulos-iberos-y-ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-misericordia-circular-desde-arens-de-lledo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entre Túmulos Iberos y Ermita de nuestra señora de La Misericordia — circular desde Arens de Lledó&lt;/a&gt;, which combines natural landscapes with historical sites. Many paths are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What natural features or scenic points can I see while mountain biking?
A: You can enjoy numerous natural features. The Algars River is a constant companion on many routes, offering natural bathing areas like El Galeró. You might also encounter the panoramic views from El Balcón del Algars, lush pine forests, and the unique Mediterranean ecosystem along the riverbanks. For a specific natural highlight, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can cycle past Iberian tumuli and the Hermitage of San Hipólito, an 18th-century hermitage with a picnic area. The Val de Zafán Greenway, an old railway line, features historical elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus)&lt;/a&gt; and impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2975160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnels on the Terra Alta Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. The village of Arens De Lledó itself has a Gothic Church and remains of an old convent.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Arens De Lledó?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the combination of gentle riverside tracks and more challenging trails, and the opportunity to explore historical sites. The accessible Val de Zafán Greenway is also a popular highlight for its smooth, scenic ride.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or difficult mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Arens De Lledó offers around 15 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995939433/calaceite-la-fresneda-y-ermita-de-santa-ana-y-los-santos-circular-por-la-comarca-de-matarrana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calaceite, La Fresneda y Ermita de Santa Ana y Los Santos — circular por la comarca de Matarraña&lt;/a&gt; provide longer distances and significant elevation gains, leading through the broader Matarraña region.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trail, many routes are accessible from Arens De Lledó or nearby towns. The Val de Zafán Greenway, for instance, often has designated access points, and the old Bot Station, now a bar, serves as a convenient stop. The Hermitage of San Hipólito also features a picnic area, suggesting accessibility for visitors.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Access to mountain bike trails in Arens De Lledó primarily relies on personal transport due to the rural nature of the Matarraña region. However, the Val de Zafán Greenway, a converted railway line, connects several towns, and some sections might be reachable via regional bus services to towns along its route, such as Bot, which has a former station now serving as a bar.
tips: At this point we find the long bridge that crosses the Algars river during the route of the Val de Zafán Greenway near the town of Lledó

A beautiful town that we can take advantage of to stop to rest on our Greenway route and where the Church of La Asunción stands out, a Gothic-Renaissance building from the 16th century or the chapel portal of San Antonio de Padua, the rest of the old walls that surrounded the population.
Near the Ermita de la Misericordia de Cretas we find these incredible views of the nearby Sierras. Without a doubt, an ideal place to take a break or take a photograph to remember.
